hazardous_situation: Caregiver does not perceive that the pump has halted infusion due to a downstream occlusion, leaving the patient without prescribed PCA medication for an extended interval.
function: Alarm propagation following safety-relevant fault detection
hazardous_sequence_of_events: Step 1: occlusion sensor trips a hardware interrupt. Step 2: alarm controller logs the event into the alarm queue. Step 3: audio-display driver fails to enqueue the alarm or fails to render it (no audible tone, no display flash). Step 4: caregiver does not perceive the interrupted infusion. Step 5: patient receives no medication for the duration of the missed alarm window.
software_related_causes: 1) Alarm queue overrun under bursty alarm conditions causing high-priority alarms to be dropped. 2) Audio/display driver race condition during boot or recovery that leaves the alarm subsystem in a no-op state without a heartbeat check.
harm_severity_rationale: Suppressed alarms during PCA infusion may delay caregiver intervention by 15+ minutes. For prescribed analgesics this typically results in moderate harm (uncontrolled pain, vital-sign deterioration); for time-critical infusions (e.g. vasopressors) it can escalate to severe harm. The dominant clinical scenario for this product is PCA pain management, so severity is graded Critical rather than Catastrophic.
harm: Patient does not receive prescribed PCA medication for an extended interval, resulting in uncontrolled pain and possible vital-sign deterioration.
exploitability_pre_mitigation: Likely under sustained alarm-burst conditions or immediately after pump restart
risk_control_measures: REQ-PUMP-201 priority-aware alarm queue with overflow backpressure to guarantee high-priority alarm delivery; REQ-PUMP-202 audio+display driver heartbeat verification with redundant alarm path on heartbeat loss.
demonstration_of_effectiveness: Burst test of 50 alarms within 100 ms confirms zero high-priority drops (TC-PUMP-201-B). Driver heartbeat fault injection confirms redundant display alarm + technician notification fires within 1 second of missed heartbeat (TC-PUMP-202-B).
exploitability_post_mitigation: Rare given verified backpressure and verified redundant alarm path
residual_risk_acceptability: Acceptable per Senior Safety Officer review on 2026-04-15

requirements.text: The alarm subsystem SHALL maintain a priority-aware alarm queue with backpressure such that high-priority alarms (occlusion, air-in-line, free-flow) are never dropped, even under sustained bursts of up to 100 alarm events per second for 1 second.

The pump SHALL verify the audio and display driver heartbeats every 500 ms and SHALL activate a redundant alarm path (display flash + technician notification) within 1 second of detecting a missed heartbeat.

test_cases.description: Single occlusion alarm propagates end-to-end (functional happy path for REQ-PUMP-201).
test_cases.setup: FluxPump 4000 powered on, alarm subsystem initialised, simulated patient line connected with downstream-occlusion test rig idle.
test_cases.steps: 1. Apply downstream occlusion via test rig. 2. Wait until occlusion sensor trips (expected within 250 ms). 3. Observe alarm controller log entry. 4. Observe audio output and display flash.
test_cases.expectedResults: Alarm queue receives exactly one high-priority occlusion event. Audible alarm tone is generated within 500 ms of sensor trip. Display flashes the occlusion banner within 500 ms of sensor trip. No queued alarms are dropped.

test_cases.description: 50-alarm burst test verifies queue backpressure preserves all high-priority alarms (boundary + negative path for REQ-PUMP-201).
test_cases.setup: FluxPump 4000 powered on, alarm subsystem initialised, instrumented alarm-injection harness connected.
test_cases.steps: 1. Inject 50 alarm events within 100 ms via the harness, of which 10 are high-priority (occlusion / air-in-line / free-flow) and 40 are low-priority (infiltration warning, low-battery). 2. Capture queue state and dropped-alarm log. 3. Repeat the burst three times back-to-back to test sustained behaviour.
test_cases.expectedResults: All 30 high-priority alarms across the three bursts are preserved in the queue. Backpressure indicator engages for low-priority alarms within 50 ms of burst start. Dropped-alarm log contains only low-priority entries; zero high-priority drops are recorded across the three bursts.

test_cases.description: Heartbeat verification fires the audio+display path on a normal alarm 1 s after audio-driver init (functional happy path for REQ-PUMP-202).
test_cases.setup: FluxPump 4000 cold-booted, audio driver init sequence completed, heartbeat verifier task running at 500 ms cadence.
test_cases.steps: 1. Wait 1 second after driver init completes. 2. Inject a single occlusion alarm via the harness. 3. Observe heartbeat-verifier log, audio output, and display.
test_cases.expectedResults: Heartbeat verifier reports both audio and display heartbeats as healthy in the most recent two cycles. Audible tone is generated within 500 ms. Display flashes the alarm banner within 500 ms. Redundant alarm path is NOT engaged because both heartbeats are healthy.

test_cases.description: Driver heartbeat fault injection triggers redundant alarm path and technician notification (negative path for REQ-PUMP-202).
test_cases.setup: FluxPump 4000 powered on, heartbeat verifier task running at 500 ms cadence, instrumented driver fault-injection layer enabled.
test_cases.steps: 1. Inject a fault that suppresses the audio driver heartbeat starting at t=0. 2. At t=600 ms inject a single high-priority alarm. 3. Capture heartbeat-verifier log, redundant-display alarm log, and technician-notification log.
test_cases.expectedResults: Heartbeat verifier flags the audio path as unhealthy within one verification cycle (≤500 ms). The redundant alarm path activates within 1 second of the missed heartbeat: display flashes the alarm banner AND a technician notification is queued. The audible tone is suppressed (expected, given the injected fault), but no patient-facing alarm is silently dropped.

design_docs.name: Alarm Subsystem Architecture
design_docs.description: Architectural overview of the FluxPump 4000 alarm subsystem including the priority-aware alarm queue, audio/display driver layering, and the redundant alarm path triggered by heartbeat-verifier failure.
